findings.details: Standard user in Marketing (Architect) holds access to critical identity systems: PROD_DB, ADMIN_SYS. These systems control authentication, authorisation, and audit logs. findings.recommendation: Raise access review ticket immediately; validate business justification with department head and CISO; remove access to PROD_DB, ADMIN_SYS unless documented exceptional approval; add to enhanced SIEM monitoring during review period

findings.details: Holds conflicting access pair [PROD_DB + ADMIN_SYS] — this violates separation of duties. With PROD_DB access, the user can modify data; with ADMIN_SYS, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.
findings.recommendation: Remove access to one of (PROD_DB, ADMIN_SYS) immediately; if business critical, add compensating control with dual-approval workflow and enhanced SIEM monitoring; document in GRC system as accepted risk with executive sign-off

findings.details: Holds conflicting access pair [SIEM + AD] — this violates separation of duties. With SIEM access, the user can modify data; with AD, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.
